hook.text: He crossed a desert to pick fruit for minimum wage. Back home, that money feeds his entire family for a month.

buildup.text: Migration is driven by global inequality. People move from poor regions to rich ones seeking opportunity.

discovery.text: Remittances — money sent home by migrants — exceed all foreign aid combined. Migration is a survival strategy.

twist.text: Receiving countries benefit from cheap labor but restrict migrants' rights. They want the work without the worker.

climax.text: Migration doesn't erase inequality. It redistributes it. Migrants gain income but lose community, safety, and status.

punchline.text: People don't leave home for fun. They leave because they must.

quiz.question: How do remittances compare to foreign aid?
options: Remittances exceed all foreign aid combined
Foreign aid is much larger than remittances
They are roughly equal in size
